description: Initialize displacement, velocity and position of all structure nodes in the range.

6.0.description: Initialize quantities on all structure nodes in the range. Note that positions can only be specified in the global system.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: position of node (global system). This is used for positioning of a structural element grid; must be used after creating the node, but before executing any cycles. Any node that is moved into a zone (see the structure link list command) will have its link deleted (if one is present) and will have a new link created with attachment conditions corresponding to the type of structural element using the node (see this table). If more than one element type is using the node, then the attachment condition will correspond with the first element type in the following list: liner, geogrid, pile, cable, shell, and beam. Thus, if a node is being used by both a geogrid and a cable, then the attachment condition after moving this node will correspond with that of a geogrid.

keywords.name: position-x
keywords.description: x-coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: position-y
keywords.description: y-coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: position-z
keywords.description: z-coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: ratio-target
keywords.description: Sets the target local force ratio that is considered to be converged for mechanical calculations. The default value is 1e-4. A local force ratio of this value will result in a convergence value of 1.0 for the node. See the convergence keyword in the model solve command.

keywords.name: local
keywords.description: specifying the supplied value will initialize the node in local coordinates. This keyword may not be used with position (position, position-x, etc.).

7.0.description: Initialize quantities on all structure nodes in the range. Note that positions can only be specified in the global system.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: position of node (global system). This is used for positioning of a structural element grid; must be used after creating the node, but before executing any cycles. Any node that is moved into a zone (see the structure link list command) will have its link deleted (if one is present) and will have a new link created with attachment conditions corresponding to the type of structural element using the node (see this table). If more than one element type is using the node, then the attachment condition will correspond with the first element type in the following list: liner, geogrid, pile, cable, shell, and beam. Thus, if a node is being used by both a geogrid and a cable, then the attachment condition after moving this node will correspond with that of a geogrid.

keywords.name: position-x
keywords.description: \(x\)-coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: position-y
keywords.description: \(y\)-coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: position-z
keywords.description: \(z\)-coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: ratio-target
keywords.description: Sets the target local force ratio that is considered to be converged for mechanical calculations. The default value is 1e-4. A local force ratio of this value will result in a convergence value of 1.0 for the node. See the convergence keyword in the model solve command.

keywords.name: add
keywords.description: the existing value of the parameter (of all nodes in the range) is set to its current value plus the given value. For example, one can add 0.1 to all node \(x\)-displacements with the command structure node initialize displacement-x 0.1 add.

keywords.name: local
keywords.description: specifying the supplied value will initialize the node in local coordinates. This keyword may not be used with position (position, position-x, etc.).

keywords.name: gradient
keywords.description: the value installed in each node in the range is \(val_f\) = \(v\) + \(xg_x\) + \(yg_y\) + \(zg_z\), where the gradient keyword is given immediately after the the supplied value (\(val\) here), and (\(x\), \(y\), \(z\)) are the components supplied by v, which specifies the reference position of the node. If the multiply keyword is also present, then the given gradient is applied to the multiplier.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: position of node (global system). This is used for positioning of structural elements, and must be used after creating the node, but before executing any cycles. A node that is moved into a zone will have its link deleted (if one is present) and will have a new link created with attachment conditions corresponding to the type of structural element using the node (see this table ). If more than one element type is using the node, then the attachment condition will correspond with the first element type in the following list: liner, geogrid, pile, cable, shell, and beam. Thus, if a node is being used by both a geogrid and a cable, then the attachment condition after moving this node will correspond with that of a geogrid.

keywords.name: position-x
keywords.description: \(x\) -coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: position-y
keywords.description: \(y\) -coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: position-z
keywords.description: \(z\) -coordinate of node (global system). See position above.

keywords.name: ratio-target
keywords.description: Sets the target local force ratio that is considered to be converged for mechanical calculations. The default value is 1e-4. A local force ratio of this value will result in a convergence value of 1.0 for the node. See the convergence keyword in the model solve command.

keywords.name: add
keywords.description: Add the specified value to the existing value.

keywords.name: gradient
keywords.description: Apply a gradient to the scalar-value provided.

keywords.name: local
keywords.description: Specify that the value supplied for the keyword is in the local coordinate system of the gridpoint (if any). By default values are initialize in the global system.

keywords.name: multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the existing value by the specified value.

keywords.name: vary
keywords.description: Apply a linear variation to the scalar-value provided.

keywords.name: acceleration
keywords.description: initialize the translational acceleration vector at nodes in the range

keywords.name: acceleration-x
keywords.description: initialize the x-component of translational acceleration at nodes in the range

keywords.name: acceleration-y
keywords.description: initialize the y-component of translational acceleration at nodes in the range

keywords.name: acceleration-z
keywords.description: initialize the z-component of translational acceleration at nodes in the range

keywords.name: acceleration-rotational
keywords.description: initialize the rotational acceleration vector at nodes in the range

keywords.name: acceleration-rotational-x
keywords.description: initialize the x-component of rotational acceleration at nodes in the range

keywords.name: acceleration-rotational-y
keywords.description: initialize the y-component of rotational acceleration at nodes in the range

keywords.name: acceleration-rotational-z
keywords.description: initialize the z-component of rotational acceleration at nodes in the range
